title: chrysanthellum filiforme
intro: Chrysanthellum filiforme, also known as False Chamomile or Yellow Daisy, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and even exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Chrysanthellum filiforme can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture, or applied topically as an infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
